description_long: This object is a tall, upright flowering plant with a single, slender green stem supporting a dense cluster of small, pale purple flowers at the top. The inflorescence is conical, with individual flowers arranged closely together, creating a spiked appearance. The leaves are elongated, lanceolate, and arranged alternately along the stem, with a smooth, slightly glossy surface. The flower cluster is composed of many small, five-petaled blossoms, each with a delicate, symmetrical form. The overall structure is slender and vertical, with the leafy stem providing support for the prominent terminal flower spike. This type of plant is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped areas, adding a touch of natural color and texture to the environment.
description: A tall, slender flowering plant with a dense, conical cluster of pale purple flowers atop a leafy green stem. The elongated leaves and spiked inflorescence give it a striking, vertical appearance.
description_view_0: The plant is seen from the front, showing the full height of the stem, the dense flower spike at the top, and the alternating lanceolate leaves.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated leaves extending outward and the layered structure of the flower cluster.
description_view_2: The plant is viewed from another angle, emphasizing the vertical alignment of the stem and the symmetrical arrangement of the leaves and flowers.
description_view_3: A slightly rotated view showing the conical flower spike and the alternating pattern of the leaves along the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially in a whorled configuration, forming symmetrical, star-shaped flowers densely packed in a terminal spike. Each flower appears to have overlapping petals, contributing to the dense, conical inflorescence.
